findings: Minor anatomical issue: the feet and strongly curved claws are somewhat oversized relative to the attached field-reference birds, but remain structurally plausible.
Minor rendering issue: the pale eye-ring is slightly brighter and more conspicuous than in most reference photos.

The isolated head and wing studies are clearly supplemental details, not extra appendages on the primary bird; the primary bird has one head, one beak, two anatomically implied wings, two legs, and one tail.
No material species-identification or text errors were found. Names, family, measurements, habitat, behavior, and visible field marks are supported by the checked sources and reference photos.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

verification_sources.title: Common Nightingale — Birds of the World
